jokes.text: And we're out of business. Good news. We're back in business. We've been hired to deliver an envelope.
jokes.setup: Company goes out of business
jokes.punchline: Immediately back in business for trivial delivery job
jokes.explanation: Classic Futurama absurdist logic - the instant reversal of fortune over something trivial. Good economy and character voice.

jokes.text: You're the finest crew I've had the honor to work with. Oh, my. We're being foreclosed upon. You're all terrible and incompetent.
jokes.setup: Professor praises crew as finest he's worked with
jokes.punchline: Immediately calls them terrible and incompetent upon bad news
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor character moment - his mood swings based on circumstances. Well-structured rapid reversal.

jokes.text: And... boned.
jokes.setup: They need $11 million to save the company
jokes.punchline: Bender's casual assessment of their hopeless situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender voice - blunt, crude assessment. Maximum economy with single word + setup.

jokes.text: Leela, Amy, pack us a lunch for our new lives under the bridge, while us men reminisce and smoke some stogies. We are not packing lunches, you walking beer commercials.
jokes.setup: Fry assigns traditional gender roles in crisis
jokes.punchline: Leela's cutting comeback calling them 'walking beer commercials'
jokes.explanation: Sharp gender commentary. 'Walking beer commercials' is a clever, specific insult that captures toxic masculinity tropes.

jokes.text: Remember Fry's idea to offer free delivery? It got us a lot of customers. We're a delivery company.
jokes.setup: Leela blames Fry's free delivery idea for their financial trouble
jokes.punchline: Fry points out they're literally a delivery company
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic - offering their core service for free is somehow a bad business idea. Fry's oblivious response fits his character.

jokes.text: We should become a commercial airline. I mean, we've got a ship, and teleporters won't be invented for another 15 years, according to that guy from the future.
jokes.setup: Amy suggests becoming an airline
jokes.punchline: References 'that guy from the future' casually in a show about the future
jokes.explanation: Clever meta joke about time travel logic in sci-fi. The casual reference to 'that guy from the future' while being in the future is nicely absurd.

jokes.text: Wow. That's actually a really good idea, for a woman.
jokes.setup: Fry acknowledges Amy's good idea
jokes.punchline: Adds casually sexist qualifier
jokes.explanation: Classic cringe comedy - Fry's casual sexism delivered obliviously. Sets up the episode's gender theme.

jokes.text: Girls of planet express calendar. Girlie calendar? Great idea, Amy.
jokes.setup: Amy suggests calendar, Professor questions it
jokes.punchline: Immediately endorses it as great idea
jokes.explanation: Straightforward male hypocrisy joke. Functional but predictable.

jokes.text: All female employees must pose nude if requested. That's discriminatory. No, it's in all our contracts. Here's mine. All female employees must pose nude if requested. Sounds fair.
jokes.setup: Leela objects to discriminatory contract clause
jokes.punchline: Bender's contract has identical discriminatory clause but he finds it fair
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic - the same discriminatory clause in everyone's contract somehow makes it fair. Bender's robot perspective adds to the humor.

jokes.text: Now that's what I call fine print.
jokes.setup: Discriminatory nude clause in contracts
jokes.punchline: Hermes' bureaucratic pride in the technicality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hermes character moment - bureaucratic pride in technically legal discrimination. Good economy and character integration.

jokes.text: Look, I agree it'd make spacewalking more comfortable. But for a photo shoot, it's a little... Unsavory.
jokes.setup: Leela in revealing spacesuit for photo shoot
jokes.punchline: Her understated objection to obviously inappropriate outfit
jokes.explanation: Leela's characteristic understatement and practicality. 'Unsavory' is amusingly mild for the situation.

jokes.text: Time is money, peaches. Now shut your com-link and make love to the camera. And remember, I'm the camera.
jokes.setup: Bender directing photo shoot professionally
jokes.punchline: Reveals his creepy personal involvement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - starts professional then reveals his pervy agenda. Good escalation structure and very quotable.

jokes.text: Stop actually washing and play with the sponges. But the ship is dirty. Eh, whatever. I ran out of film an hour ago.
jokes.setup: Bender directing Amy to pose rather than actually clean
jokes.punchline: Reveals he wasn't even filming for an hour
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - Bender's lazy deception and Amy's innocent work ethic. The hour-long lie is perfectly Bender.

jokes.text: A three month calendar? What is this, mercury?
jokes.setup: They only have enough women for a short calendar
jokes.punchline: Mercury reference (shortest orbital period)
jokes.explanation: Clever sci-fi astronomy joke. Mercury has shortest year, so shortest calendar. Good nerdy Futurama humor.

jokes.text: Fortunately, I came up with a brilliant idea to save Planet Express. We'll turn it into a commercial airline. But that was my idea. Then you should have talked louder.
jokes.setup: Professor claims Amy's airline idea as his own
jokes.punchline: Dismisses her with 'should have talked louder' (repeated)
jokes.explanation: Running gag of Professor stealing credit. The repeated 'talk louder' dismissal becomes a mini running gag.

jokes.text: There. It was hard work, but it beats posing in demeaning, skimpy modeling outfits. Ladies, here are your demeaning, skimpy stewardess outfits.
jokes.setup: Leela relieved to avoid demeaning modeling outfits
jokes.punchline: Immediately presented with equally demeaning stewardess outfits
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff timing. The identical adjectives 'demeaning, skimpy' make the irony clear and punchy.

jokes.text: Ladies and gentlemen, this is plane's president speaking. You put goofus and ganja in charge? I'm the only trained pilot here. Oh, please, Leela. Who ever heard of a plane with a woman president?
jokes.setup: Fry announces himself as 'plane's president'
jokes.punchline: Professor's sexist dismissal of female leadership
jokes.explanation: The 'plane's president' wordplay is clever, and Professor's casual sexism fits the episode's theme.

jokes.text: The local time on acapulsar is five years from now. In the event of a wormhole sending us back in time, do not kill your parents. If you are traveling with small children, help them not to kill you before not killing your own parents.
jokes.setup: Standard airline safety announcement format
jokes.punchline: Increasingly convoluted time travel paradox warnings
jokes.explanation: Brilliant parody of airline safety announcements with sci-fi twist. The escalating complexity of the time paradox warnings is genuinely creative.

jokes.text: I'll kill you, my friend!
jokes.setup: Plane experiencing turbulence
jokes.punchline: Passenger's contradictory threat to friend
jokes.explanation: Simple contradiction humor - threatening to kill someone while calling them friend. Good economy but not particularly original.

jokes.text: So what's the deal with airline food, hmm? They offer you two choices, but they're always out of one. By the way, we're out of both choices.
jokes.setup: Classic airline food complaint setup
jokes.punchline: They're out of both choices, making it worse than the cliché
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-comedy that acknowledges the cliché airline food joke then makes it worse. Good escalation from standard complaint to absurd reality.

jokes.text: Shut up and put on four episodes of The Office.
jokes.setup: Passenger wants entertainment instead of bad comedy
jokes.punchline: Specific reference to binge-watching The Office
jokes.explanation: Basic pop culture reference. Relies on recognition of The Office's popularity rather than actual comedic construction.

jokes.text: Ooh, your hands are warm. For a robot.
jokes.setup: LaBarbara compliments Bender's warm hands
jokes.punchline: Qualifier that robots shouldn't have warm hands
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor about robot physiology. The qualifier 'for a robot' adds the comedic twist to simple compliment.

jokes.text: Thanks, baby. I don't know what you're doing back here in the galley, 'cause you got a first class seat.
jokes.setup: Bender flirting with LaBarbara in galley
jokes.punchline: She has first class seat but he's hitting on her in economy section
jokes.explanation: Bender's smooth-talking character and the implication of airline class disparity. Decent character moment.

jokes.text: Captain, we were supposed to land two days ago. Is everything... hey! Are we at the fair yet, mommy? We're not at the any place. And we're out of fuel.
jokes.setup: Flight supposed to land two days ago
jokes.punchline: Child's innocent question reveals they're completely lost and out of fuel
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from late arrival to complete navigation failure. Child's innocent question contrasts with dire situation.

jokes.text: Attention, passengers. Please remain seated until the plane comes to a complete crash.
jokes.setup: Standard airline landing announcement format
jokes.punchline: Substitutes 'crash' for 'stop' in airline announcement template
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and structure. Perfect parody of airline announcements with single word substitution that completely changes meaning. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Flight attendant's log: We've crash near a river of mercury on a lifeless mineral world. Supplies are low, cannibalism imminent, radio busted.
jokes.setup: Professional flight attendant log format
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions cannibalism alongside other problems
jokes.explanation: Good escalation in the list of problems, with 'cannibalism imminent' being the darkly funny peak. Professional tone contrasts with dire content.

jokes.text: All in favor of the jamocha thunderclap? That would be me. Practically no one.
jokes.setup: LaBarbara proposes herself as leader with elaborate name
jokes.punchline: Only gets her own vote and understates the failure
jokes.explanation: 'Jamocha thunderclap' is a wonderfully absurd self-designation, and her understatement of total failure is good character work.

jokes.text: We can't trust a woman's math. Count again.
jokes.setup: Vote results in 8-8 tie
jokes.punchline: Professor's casually sexist dismissal of basic counting
jokes.explanation: Fits episode's gender theme. Professor's sexism about basic arithmetic is absurdly dismissive.

jokes.text: Leader? Fat man, you couldn't even run a bath. Well, you sure can run something-- your mouth!
jokes.setup: LaBarbara insults Professor's leadership with 'couldn't run a bath'
jokes.punchline: Professor's comeback about running her mouth
jokes.explanation: Standard insult exchange. 'Couldn't run a bath' is a classic put-down, Professor's comeback is predictable but serviceable.

jokes.text: Gender? Yeahs. You knows guyses, galses, in that order. Ows.
jokes.setup: Alien asks about gender concept
jokes.punchline: Bender's crude explanation with apparent physical consequence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender voice - crude, direct, and apparently gets hurt explaining basic concepts. The 'in that order' shows his bias.

jokes.text: Interesting. Come on, men. Let's kill him.
jokes.setup: Alien expresses academic interest in gender
jokes.punchline: Fry's immediate violent response to intellectual curiosity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and character work - Fry's anti-intellectual masculine posturing. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: Chocolate, Mac, men. The end.
jokes.setup: Alien lists pairs where one is better: chocolate/vanilla, Mac/PC
jokes.punchline: Fry confidently declares men superior in same format
jokes.explanation: Good structure using the established pattern. Fry's confident dismissal fits his character. 'The end' shows his certainty.

jokes.text: Which is larger, an Italian size four or an American apparel medium? How many calories are in a small green tea yogurt? Name any 12 of the desperate housewives. How was your day?
jokes.setup: Series of stereotypically feminine knowledge questions
jokes.punchline: Final question is the most stereotypically relationship-oriented
jokes.explanation: Good escalation through stereotypical 'women's knowledge' ending with relationship communication. Relies somewhat on gender stereotypes but serves the episode's theme.

jokes.text: Uh... men five, women less. Hey. He's lying. Obviously. That was a test to see who could lie better. The men win that round.
jokes.setup: Bender gives obviously false score favoring men
jokes.punchline: Alien reveals it was a test of lying ability, men win
jokes.explanation: Clever multi-layered joke. Bender's obvious lie becomes the actual test, and the meta-nature of testing lying through lying is well-constructed.

jokes.text: In your face, decumbent urinators.
jokes.setup: Fry celebrates men winning the lying test
jokes.punchline: Uses 'decumbent urinators' as insult for women
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd insult. 'Decumbent urinators' is technically accurate for how women urinate but sounds like fancy scientific terminology used as schoolyard taunt. Very creative and quotable.

jokes.text: Did one of you chicks change the thermostat?
jokes.setup: Planet getting hotter as they approach sun
jokes.punchline: Fry blames women for cosmic thermostat change
jokes.explanation: Excellent domestic stereotype applied to cosmic scale. Perfect Fry logic - blaming women for astronomical events. Great economy and character integration.

jokes.text: You will all be boiled alive, like retired circus animals
jokes.setup: Describing how they'll die from heat
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and dark comparison to retired circus animals
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully dark and specific comparison. The detail about 'retired' circus animals makes it more absurd and memorable. Classic Futurama dark humor.

jokes.text: And may the inferior gender burst into something. Flames, I guess.
jokes.setup: Dramatic declaration about inferior gender's fate
jokes.punchline: Uncertain, casual suggestion of 'flames, I guess'
jokes.explanation: Good anticlimax - builds dramatic tension then deflates with uncertain 'I guess.' The casual tone contrasts with dire prediction.

jokes.text: Howdy there. You fellas need some directions? No, we're fine. Don't worry about it.
jokes.setup: Someone offers help with directions to obviously lost group
jokes.punchline: Fry refuses help with classic male stubbornness
jokes.explanation: Classic masculine stereotype about refusing to ask for directions. Well-executed character moment for Fry, fits episode theme.

jokes.text: Oh, look! A clearance sale! What?! Where?
jokes.setup: Women struggling through desert survival
jokes.punchline: Immediately distracted by mirage of shopping
jokes.explanation: Relies on shopping stereotype for women. Functional but plays into predictable gender clichés without much creativity.

jokes.text: It wasn't Tommy hilfijigger. It was linens 'n things! The hell it was. It was a juicy couture. I guess we all saw what we wanted to see.
jokes.setup: Women arguing about what store they saw in mirage
jokes.punchline: Leela's wise observation about seeing what they wanted
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of brand arguments and Leela's philosophical conclusion. 'Tommy hilfijigger' is a nice name mangle.

jokes.text: So if one's got any good fart jokes, now would be the time. It's a shmundred degrees, and all we have to drink are non-diet sodas.
jokes.setup: Facing death, Fry asks for fart jokes
jokes.punchline: Amy's complaint about non-diet sodas ruining her figure
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moments - Fry's juvenile humor and Amy's vanity priorities. 'Shmundred' is a nice Fry mispronunciation.

jokes.text: It, I forgot that amana here is a refrigerator. If we could supercharge her freon with a gas compressor, we might survive the heat and make to the cave. Hey, yeah! But the only gas compressor on this planet is inside bender's ass.
jokes.setup: Technical solution using refrigerator and gas compressor
jokes.punchline: Gas compressor is specifically Bender's digestive system
jokes.explanation: Good sci-fi technical solution that becomes absurd robot anatomy joke. Connects character abilities to survival needs cleverly.

jokes.text: Was that a fart joke? Because I don't find those amusing. No one does.
jokes.setup: Reference to Bender's gas compressor
jokes.punchline: Amy's denial that fart jokes are funny, followed by universal dismissal
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on low-brow humor. Amy's prim dismissal followed by 'no one does' is good character work and self-aware.

jokes.text: None of your bizmarkie. What's that doo-wah in your ditty bag?
jokes.setup: LaBarbara being evasive about her actions
jokes.punchline: Both using musical/slang terms instead of normal words
jokes.explanation: Good character voice - both using hip-hop/musical terminology. 'Bizmarkie' and 'doo-wah' fit their musical background.

jokes.text: Damn it, woman, you're making me highly selassie!
jokes.setup: Angry confrontation with wife
jokes.punchline: Using Rastafarian emperor name as emotional descriptor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Hermes-specific wordplay combining Jamaican culture with emotion. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: How do you live with yourself? Damn it, woman, you're making me highly selassie! Oh, don't you talk to me that way, you big, hairy Belafonte. Oh, you're cookin me back bacon. Thank you, woman. That's not back-bacon! That's your back bakin'!
jokes.setup: Couple arguing about stealing cooling equipment
jokes.punchline: Series of Jamaican/musical references used as insults and terms of endearment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-specific argument using musical/Jamaican references as both insults and endearments. 'Highly Selassie' and 'big hairy Belafonte' are creative. The back-bacon/back bakin' pun is excellent.

jokes.text: That's not back-bacon! That's your back bakin'!
jokes.setup: Heat causing physical cooking
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'back bacon' and literal back baking
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay combining food pun with actual dangerous situation

jokes.text: My wingwang's gone! My girls! My antennae! My kajigger! My gonopores!
jokes.setup: Characters discovering their genitals are gone
jokes.punchline: Each character's unique anatomical terminology
jokes.explanation: Creative character-specific euphemisms escalating to alien anatomy

jokes.text: Work goes fasters withouts no secondary sex characteristicses at which to hoot.
jokes.setup: Everyone working well together without gender differences
jokes.punchline: Bender's crude but accurate observation about workplace distraction
jokes.explanation: Good Bender voice - crude but technically accurate social observation. 'Characteristicses' and 'hoot' are perfect Bender vocabulary.

jokes.text: Yes, this place is like a neutered utopia. A neutopia.
jokes.setup: Describing their peaceful genderless society
jokes.punchline: Creates portmanteau 'neutopia' from neuter + utopia
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that gives the episode its title. Good economy and the pun works both linguistically and thematically.

jokes.text: Really, what are we missing out on by not having sex right now? Well...
jokes.setup: Hermes asks what they're missing without sexual desire
jokes.punchline: Someone starts to answer then trails off
jokes.explanation: Good setup for awkward moment. The trailing off suggests someone was about to give TMI answer, creating uncomfortable beat.

jokes.text: It's about loving, and fighting, and that rasta mcnasty we were doing last night.
jokes.setup: Fry explaining what makes being human meaningful
jokes.punchline: Crude euphemism for sexual activity
jokes.explanation: 'Rasta mcnasty' is a wonderfully absurd euphemism. Perfect Fry voice - crude but delivered with earnest emotion about human experience.

jokes.text: Not cool, bro! Our genders are all reversed. Now I got a whatchacallit instead of a kajigger, you stupid whatchacallit!
jokes.setup: Bender discovers his gender has been reversed
jokes.punchline: Uses made-up euphemisms then calls alien same euphemism as insult
jokes.explanation: Good character work - Bender's crude vocabulary and logic. Using 'whatchacallit' as both body part reference and insult is cleverly circular.

jokes.text: Well, hello from the neck down.
jokes.setup: Zapp arrives to help with gender-swapped crew
jokes.punchline: His typical sleazy pickup line adapted to unusual situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp line - sleazy and inappropriate even in bizarre circumstances. Great character consistency and economy.

jokes.text: I gotta get up five times a night to Xbox.
jokes.setup: LaBarbara (now male) explaining sleep disruption
jokes.punchline: Uses 'Xbox' as verb for male bathroom habits
jokes.explanation: Brilliant and unexpected use of 'Xbox' as euphemism for urination. Creative, economical, and fits the futuristic setting while being immediately understandable.

jokes.text: For example, did you know there are more than two feelings?
jokes.setup: Hermes (now female) learning about womanhood
jokes.punchline: Discovery that emotional range is broader than expected
jokes.explanation: Excellent observation about gender stereotypes and emotional awareness. Perfect for formerly-male character discovering broader emotional range.

jokes.text: O.M.G.! Being chicks is so much fun. Now when I say stupid things, guys all laugh and buy me stuff.
jokes.setup: Fry (now female) enjoying new gender experience
jokes.punchline: Cynical observation about how men treat attractive women
jokes.explanation: Good social observation delivered with Fry's characteristic obliviousness. The 'O.M.G.' fits the gender-swap performance.

jokes.text: Ugh. What a skank.
jokes.setup: Fry enjoying stereotypical feminine behavior
jokes.punchline: Leela's disgusted reaction to his performance
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing Leela's feminist perspective on Fry's gender performance. Economical but effective.

jokes.text: Thank God most of our fans are huge perverts.
jokes.setup: Calendar sales saved the company
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging their audience's motivations
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on both the show's audience and calendar market. Self-aware and economical.

jokes.text: I don't care what parts I have, as long as they interlock with yours.
jokes.setup: Leela asking about Fry's gender preference
jokes.punchline: Sweet romantic sentiment using mechanical metaphor
jokes.explanation: Surprisingly sweet romantic line using 'interlock' as both mechanical and sexual metaphor. Perfect Fry - crude but genuine emotion.

jokes.text: It combines the contentedness of being neutered, with the occasional sex of being not.
jokes.setup: Professor commenting on marriage
jokes.punchline: Clinical analysis of marriage as compromise between sexual frustration and satisfaction
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly clinical and pessimistic view of marriage. The contrast between 'neutered' and 'occasional sex' is darkly funny and honest.

jokes.text: You folks watch your genitals now.
jokes.setup: Borax Kid giving farewell advice
jokes.punchline: Treating genitals like valuable possessions to be protected
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the gender transformation plot. Treating genitals like valuables in folksy Western advice is appropriately absurd.

jokes.text: You folks watch your genitals now.
jokes.setup: Standard farewell from departing character
jokes.punchline: Warning specifically about genitals given episode events
jokes.explanation: Good callback that subverts standard farewell with episode-specific warning
